Projet 4 : Validation croisée et Random Forests

Margot Laignel-Arthur Hauguel-Loïc Mémeteau

Chargement des Données

library(randomForest)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.

load("bacteria-dataset.Rdata")
summary(X)

##  3rd Qu.:0.07656   3rd Qu.:0.07652   3rd Qu.:0.07628   3rd Qu.:0.07627  
##  Max.   :0.10091   Max.   :0.09848   Max.   :0.10007   Max.   :0.10010
dim(X)
## [1] 2403  240
# Visualisation
matplot(t(X), type = "l", lty = 1, col = ifelse(Y == 1, "orange", "grey"),
        main = "Signaux spectraux (Orange = Classe 1, Gris = Classe 0)",
        xlab = "Canal spectral", ylab = "Intensité")

parametres_ntree <- c(25, 50, 100, 250, 500, 1000)
kfolds <- 10
# Classe 1 : positive
# Classe 0 : negative
accuracy <- function(true_positive, true_negative, false_positive, false_negative) {
  # Nombre total d'observations evaluees.
  total <- true_positive + true_negative + false_positive + false_negative
  # Evite une division par zero si jamais un fold est vide.
  if (total == 0) return(NA_real_)
  (true_positive + true_negative) / total
}

sensitivity <- function(true_positive, false_negative) {
  # Sensibilite = proportion de positifs correctement detectes.
  denom <- true_positive + false_negative
  if (denom == 0) return(NA_real_)
  true_positive / denom
}

specificity <- function(true_negative, false_positive) {
  # Specificite = proportion de negatifs correctement detectes.
  denom <- true_negative + false_positive
  if (denom == 0) return(NA_real_)
  true_negative / denom
}

results <- function(y_true, y_pred) {
  # Uniformise le type pour comparer proprement "0"/"1" (facteurs, numeriques...).
  y_true <- as.character(y_true)
  y_pred <- as.character(y_pred)

  # Composantes de la matrice de confusion (classe positive = "1").
  true_positive <- sum(y_true == "1" & y_pred == "1")
  false_positive <- sum(y_true == "0" & y_pred == "1")
  true_negative <- sum(y_true == "0" & y_pred == "0")
  false_negative <- sum(y_true == "1" & y_pred == "0")

  acc <- accuracy(true_positive, true_negative, false_positive, false_negative)
  sens <- sensitivity(true_positive, false_negative)
  spec <- specificity(true_negative, false_positive)

  c(accuracy = acc, sensitivity = sens, specificity = spec)
}
# Création de 10 folds stratifies (pour conserver la proportion des classes)
set.seed(123)
y_char <- as.character(Y)

# On melange separement les indices des positifs et des negatifs.
idx_pos <- sample(which(y_char == "1"))
idx_neg <- sample(which(y_char == "0"))

fold_id <- integer(length(Y))
# Répartition des positifs dans les 10 folds.
fold_id[idx_pos] <- rep(seq_len(kfolds), length.out = length(idx_pos))
# Répartition des négatifs dans les 10 folds.
fold_id[idx_neg] <- rep(seq_len(kfolds), length.out = length(idx_neg))

# Liste des indices de test pour chaque fold.
folds <- lapply(seq_len(kfolds), function(f) which(fold_id == f))
# Boucle de validation croisee sur les valeurs de ntree
# Contiendra, pour chaque ntree, les k splits (train/test) associés.
cv_splits <- vector("list", length(parametres_ntree))
names(cv_splits) <- paste0("ntree_", parametres_ntree)

for (i in seq_along(parametres_ntree)) {
  ntree_value <- parametres_ntree[i]
  cv_splits[[i]] <- vector("list", kfolds)

  for (fold in seq_len(kfolds)) {
    test_idx <- folds[[fold]]
    # Tous les indices sauf ceux de test servent a l'entrainement.
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), test_idx)

    cv_splits[[i]][[fold]] <- list(
      ntree = ntree_value,
      fold = fold,
      train_idx = train_idx,
      test_idx = test_idx
    )
  }
}
rf1 <- randomForest(x = X, y = as.factor(Y))
summary(rf1)
##                 Length Class  Mode     
## call               3   -none- call     
## type               1   -none- character
## predicted       2403   factor numeric  
## err.rate        1500   -none- numeric  
## confusion          6   -none- numeric  
## votes           4806   matrix numeric  
## oob.times       2403   -none- numeric  
## classes            2   -none- character
## importance       240   -none- numeric  
## importanceSD       0   -none- NULL     
## localImportance    0   -none- NULL     
## proximity          0   -none- NULL     
## ntree              1   -none- numeric  
## mtry               1   -none- numeric  
## forest            14   -none- list     
## y               2403   factor numeric  
## test               0   -none- NULL     
## inbag              0   -none- NULL
stats <- results(Y, rf1$predicted)
print(stats)
##    accuracy sensitivity specificity 
##   0.9625468   0.9219858   0.9749864
# 1. Initialisation d'un tableau pour stocker les moyennes
rf_perf <- matrix(0, nrow = length(parametres_ntree), ncol = 3)
colnames(rf_perf) <- c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity")
rownames(rf_perf) <- parametres_ntree

# 2. Boucle principale
for (i in seq_along(parametres_ntree)) {
  n <- parametres_ntree[i]

  # On utilise 'sapply' pour boucler sur les 10 folds et récupérer les résultats
  scores_folds <- sapply(1:kfolds, function(f) {
    # Indices train/test
    test_idx <- folds[[f]]
    train_idx <- setdiff(1:nrow(X), test_idx)

    # Entraînement et Prédiction
    mod <- randomForest(X[train_idx, ], as.factor(Y[train_idx]), ntree = n)
    preds <- predict(mod, X[test_idx, ])

    # Calcul des métriques via ta fonction
    return(results(Y[test_idx], preds))
  })

  # On moyenne les résultats des 10 folds (rowMeans car sapply renvoie une matrice 3x10)
  rf_perf[i, ] <- rowMeans(scores_folds)
}

# 3. Affichage final
rf_perf
##       accuracy sensitivity specificity
## 25   0.9529580   0.8986842   0.9695444
## 50   0.9608660   0.9165414   0.9744417
## 100  0.9604493   0.9147556   0.9744417
## 250  0.9608643   0.9147243   0.9749822
## 500  0.9592045   0.9130013   0.9733547
## 1000 0.9600326   0.9165414   0.9733517
# Validation croisee KNN pour K = 1,...,9
parametres_k <- 1:9
knn_perf <- matrix(0, nrow = length(parametres_k), ncol = 3)
colnames(knn_perf) <- c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity")
rownames(knn_perf) <- parametres_k

for (i in seq_along(parametres_k)) {
  k <- parametres_k[i]

  scores_folds <- sapply(seq_len(kfolds), function(f) {
    test_idx <- folds[[f]]
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), test_idx)

    # Standardisation par fold (sans fuite d'information vers le test)
    x_train <- scale(X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    center_train <- attr(x_train, "scaled:center")
    scale_train <- attr(x_train, "scaled:scale")
    scale_train[scale_train == 0] <- 1
    x_test <- scale(X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE], center = center_train, scale = scale_train)

    preds <- class::knn(train = x_train, test = x_test, cl = as.factor(Y[train_idx]), k = k)
    results(Y[test_idx], preds)
  })

  knn_perf[i, ] <- rowMeans(scores_folds)
}

knn_perf
##    accuracy sensitivity specificity
## 1 0.9716959   0.9574248   0.9760810
## 2 0.9625413   0.9413847   0.9690069
## 3 0.9675240   0.9502193   0.9728083
## 4 0.9616959   0.9395677   0.9684604
## 5 0.9666924   0.9519737   0.9711838
## 6 0.9642011   0.9414160   0.9711808
## 7 0.9671125   0.9503133   0.9722648
## 8 0.9658678   0.9467732   0.9717243
## 9 0.9637879   0.9485902   0.9684604
# Figure KNN: evolution des performances en fonction de K
matplot(
  x = parametres_k,
  y = knn_perf,
  type = "b",
  pch = 1:3,
  lty = 1,
  col = c("dodgerblue3", "darkorange3", "darkgreen"),
  xlab = "K",
  ylab = "Score moyen en validation croisee",
  main = "KNN : performances en fonction de K"
)
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity"),
  col = c("dodgerblue3", "darkorange3", "darkgreen"),
  lty = 1,
  pch = 1:3,
  bty = "n"
)

# Comparaison graphique RF vs KNN (sur les meilleures valeurs d'hyperparametres)
best_ntree <- as.numeric(rownames(rf_perf)[which.max(rf_perf[, "accuracy"])])
best_k <- as.numeric(rownames(knn_perf)[which.max(knn_perf[, "accuracy"])])

comparaison <- rbind(
  RF = rf_perf[as.character(best_ntree), ],
  KNN = knn_perf[as.character(best_k), ]
)

barplot(
  t(comparaison),
  beside = TRUE,
  col = c("steelblue3", "tomato3"),
  ylab = "Score moyen en validation croisee",
  main = paste0("Comparaison RF (ntree=", best_ntree, ") vs KNN (K=", best_k, ")")
)
legend("bottomright", legend = c("RF", "KNN"), fill = c("steelblue3", "tomato3"), bty = "n")

# Impact de mtry (avec le meilleur ntree trouve precedemment)
default_mtry <- floor(sqrt(ncol(X)))
mtry_values <- sort(unique(c(5, 10, default_mtry, 20, 30, 50)))
mtry_values <- mtry_values[mtry_values >= 1 & mtry_values <= ncol(X)]

mtry_perf <- matrix(0, nrow = length(mtry_values), ncol = 3)
colnames(mtry_perf) <- c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity")
rownames(mtry_perf) <- mtry_values

for (i in seq_along(mtry_values)) {
  m <- mtry_values[i]

  scores_folds <- sapply(seq_len(kfolds), function(f) {
    test_idx <- folds[[f]]
    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), test_idx)

    mod <- randomForest(
      x = X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE],
      y = as.factor(Y[train_idx]),
      ntree = best_ntree,
      mtry = m
    )
    preds <- predict(mod, X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    results(Y[test_idx], preds)
  })

  mtry_perf[i, ] <- rowMeans(scores_folds)
}

mtry_perf
##     accuracy sensitivity specificity
## 5  0.9612896   0.9289787   0.9711808
## 10 0.9583677   0.9130326   0.9722648
## 15 0.9608660   0.9165100   0.9744417
## 20 0.9571177   0.9058897   0.9728083
## 30 0.9537861   0.9005639   0.9700849
## 50 0.9542166   0.8970551   0.9717213
matplot(
  x = mtry_values,
  y = mtry_perf,
  type = "b",
  pch = 1:3,
  lty = 1,
  col = c("dodgerblue3", "darkorange3", "darkgreen"),
  xlab = "mtry",
  ylab = "Score moyen en validation croisee",
  main = paste0("Random Forest : impact de mtry (ntree=", best_ntree, ")")
)
abline(v = default_mtry, lty = 2, col = "grey40")
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("accuracy", "sensitivity", "specificity", paste0("mtry defaut=", default_mtry)),
  col = c("dodgerblue3", "darkorange3", "darkgreen", "grey40"),
  lty = c(1, 1, 1, 2),
  pch = c(1, 2, 3, NA),
  bty = "n"
)
